title: Backup and Archive Before Refactoring
rules: Before any significant code change, create a dated backup: cp file file.backup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M).
Keep only the latest working version in the active directory. Move old versions to archive/.
Archive old files by category: archive/main_py_backups/, archive/html_backups/, archive/scripts/.
Do not delete old files until the new version is tested and deployed.
Test syntax with python3 -m py_compile before restarting the server.
If refactoring breaks functionality, rollback from the most recent backup immediately.
Having 20+ version copies in the working directory creates chaos. One active file + organized archive.
rationale: Night session taught us: 20+ main.py versions in app/ is chaos. Organized backups save time and nerves.
examples: Before refactoring main.py: cp main.py main.py.backup-20260429-001. After success: mv old versions to archive/main_py_backups/.

anketa_context.session_summary: Learned to backup before changes after dealing with 20+ main.py versions.
